title: The Conversion Of Aurelian McGoggin
content:
Ride with an idle whip, ride with an unused heel,
But, once in a way, there will come a day
When the colt must be taught to feel
The lash that falls, and the curb that galls, and the sting
of the rowelled steel.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Rudyard Kipling delivers a powerful performance in "The Conversion Of Aurelian McGoggin"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
